text.title: Sentry Supabase Vercel Wiring Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `Sentry Supabase Vercel Wiring` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Repo MUST contain three Sentry configs: `sentry.client.config.ts`, `sentry.server.config.ts`, `sentry.edge.config.ts`. Single-config setups miss edge-runtime errors (middleware, edge API routes). Missing any file = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Next.js runs three different runtimes; Sentry's bundler-plugin emits separate transports. A single config silently drops edge-runtime errors — exactly where middleware-level auth fails.

rule.statement: Source maps MUST upload via Sentry's Next.js bundler plugin during the Vercel build (NOT a post-deploy hook). `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`, `SENTRY_ORG`, `SENTRY_PROJECT` MUST live in Vercel project env (NOT in committed code).
rule.rationale: Post-deploy hooks race the first user error. Vercel build is the only place that has both the unminified bundle and the deploy ID; everything else is hope.

rule.statement: `beforeSend` MUST scrub Supabase auth error payloads: strip `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `provider_token`, full email body, any `Authorization:` header echo.
rule.rationale: Supabase client errors include the request that failed, which often contains the JWT. Sentry then becomes the leak. Scrubbing is mandatory, not optional.

rule.statement: Supabase `PGRST*` and `42501` (RLS denial) errors MUST be classified before sending to Sentry: legitimate denials tagged `level: warning`, unexpected denials tagged `level: error`, both with `meta.expected_rls` payload.
rule.rationale: Treating every RLS denial as an error floods Sentry with user-typo noise and hides the actual bugs. Classification is what makes Supabase + Sentry usable together.

rule.statement: `sentry.edge.config.ts` MUST set `tracesSampleRate` ≤ 0.1 (10%) regardless of client/server settings.
rule.rationale: Solo plans have small spans quotas. Edge middleware runs on every request including bots — leaving sampling at 1.0 burns through monthly quota by lunchtime.

rule.statement: `ignoreErrors` MUST include the baseline noise list: ResizeObserver loop limit, AbortError on navigation, NextRouter cancel, hydration mismatch from extensions.
rule.rationale: Solo builders fight Sentry signal-to-noise for weeks before discovering the baseline list; codifying it saves the discovery cost and stops 'Sentry is useless' abandonment.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume cycles and produce false confidence.
